calendar.matters:
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Trial
Off Calendar Nov-03-2021 Off Calendar

Petition To Determine Rights Of Omitted Spouse; To Modify Trust Based Upon Changed Circumstances And Mistake; And To Determine Community Property Righ
Off Calendar Nov-03-2021 Off Calendar

Petition For Order Authorizing And Directing Transfer Of Property From Surviving Spouse To Deceased Spouses Estate
Off Calendar Nov-03-2021 Off Calendar

Petition To Approve Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Nov-09-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James Lamping, via ZOOM, for Susan Hogeland, Beneficiary, and specially appearing for Alicia Gamez, former attorney for Susan Hogeland; Richard Lund, via ZOOM, for James Mazza and Thomas Mazza, Beneficiaries; Ross Madden, via ZOOM, former attorney for Susan Hogeland.

The court points counsel to notice issues of the amended petitions in each matter where notice was provided prior to the petitions having been formally filed. The court waives the notice defects and asks if anyone has an objection. None are presented. The court advises that it is taking the petitions for each matter under submission with the intent to grant. In open court, the three (3) matters currently set in PTR-19-303320 for Nov-09-2021 are taken off calendar. Mr. Lamping asks if the court will consider continuing the Accounting Petition currently set in the estate matter for Nov-30-2021. The court asks Mr. Lamping if he has given notice regarding his request. Mr. Lamping replies no. The court denies Mr. Lamping's request.

The First Amended Petitions to Approve Settlement Agreement are submitted.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Case Management Hearing
Off Calendar Apr-06-2021 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James Lamping, via ZOOM, counsel for Susan Hogeland, Petitioner; Richard Lund, via ZOOM, counsel for Objectors James Mazza and Tom Mazza.

At the outset, the court focuses counsel on the Court's order dated SEP-20-2020 which clearly sets both petitions in the matter for trial. The court addresses the idea of the court appointing a evidence code 730 expert. The court also advises that a witness list has yet to submitted and is a concern to the court because of the impending date for trial. Mr. Lamping advises that he believed that only one petition was set for trial which is why witness lists were not submitted to the court. Mr. Lamping also believes that the appointment of a 730 expert might lead the parties to resolve this matter instead of going to trial. The court asks Mr. Lamping how the 730 expert would be funded. Mr. Lamping advises that the cost of hiring the 730 expert would be shared by the parties and would not impact the court. Mr. Lund advises that perhaps hiring a 730 expert at this moment may not be prudent because of the impending trial date and perhaps mediation may be a better option at this time again, due to the impending trial date. Mr. Lund also advises that completing accounting has been difficult due to issues getting financial records from several financial institutions. Mr. Lamping advises that he has concerns with the current trial date because of the lack of availability of the mediator (Ret. Judge Rieser) and with the lack of records from the financial institutions and hopes to continue the current trial date. The court does not want to continue the trial date at this moment and encourages the parties to meet and confer with regard to the trial date. The court will consider moving the current trial date but only if the parties stipulate to doing so or via ex parte application. After further discussion, both petitions in this matter are continued to JUN-15-2021.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn; Clerk: Robert Woods; Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. Not Reported.

Notice Of Hearing On Demurrer And Demurrer To Petition For Order Authorizing And Directing Transfer Of Property From Surviving Spouse To Deceased Spouse'S Estate
Off Calendar Mar-26-2021 Text Ruling

Petition For Order Authorizing And Directing Transfer Of Property From Surviving Spouse To Deceased Spouses Estate
Off Calendar Jan-19-2021 Continued To Apr-06-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances; James Lamping appeared by zoom for Susan Hogeland (Surviving Spouse).
Brynne Wilson and Richard Lund appeared by zoom for Thomas Mazza and James Mazza (Respondent).

The Court stated that it received the joint case management statement ("CMS"), thanked the parties, stated that it was very helpful, that at the last hearing there was some discussion about discovery and that there is some concern about the progress of discovery. Mr. Lamping stated that today is the first hearing on the Brothers' petition, that his Client filed a demurrer to the Brothers' petition with a hearing date of March 26th and that the parties have exchanged some documents. Mr. Lamping also stated that the Brothers have subpoenaed bank records, that there is a huge amount that needs to be reconstructed, but they are making progress/moving forward, raised the issue of both petitions needing to go to trial and asked the Court what it wants to do. The Court noted that the issue raised by Mr. Lamping was not teed up in the CMS, informed Counsel that petitions in probate will not be around for five years, and stated that the case management order instructed the parties how to request a continuance and that the parties should have requested it if that's what they are seeking. Mr. Lund stated that he has nothing to add, that at the next case management conference they should be in a position to see where this is going and at what point discovery is, and that the case is still on track. Ms. Wilson stated that she has nothing to add. The Petition to Determine Rights of Omitted Spouse, etc. and the Petition for Order Authorizing and Directing Transfer of Property from Surviving Spouse to Deceased Spouse's Estate are continued to April 6, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition To Determine Rights Of Omitted Spouse; To Modify Trust Based Upon Changed Circumstances And Mistake; And To Determine Community Property Righ
Off Calendar Sep-29-2020 Continued To Jan-19-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James Lamping appeared by zoom for Susan Hogeland (Surviving Spouse).
Richard Lund and Brynne Wilson appeared by zoom for Thomas Mazza and James Mazza.

The Court thanked the Parties for providing the joint status statement. Mr. Lamping stated that there is no update, that the Parties have a similar vision of where this will go and that they will need to forensically reconstruct the finances for seventeen years. Mr. lamping also stated that they will need to do some third-party discovery, that once they are done with discovery they will see where they are with respect to mediation and that perhaps they could have the Court appoint a 730 Referee, or something along those lines. Mr. Lund stated that he is on the same page. The Court informed the Parties that it will issue a further case management order. The Petition to Determine Rights of Ommitted Spouse, etc. is continued to January 19, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Case management hearings are set for January 19, 2021 and April 6, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The trial is set for June 15, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Case Management Conference
Off Calendar Sep-29-2020 Continued To Jan-19-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Determine Rights Of Omitted Spouse; To Modify Trust Based Upon Changed Circumstances And Mistake; And To Determine Community Property Righ
Off Calendar Jun-30-2020 Continued To Sep-29-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James Lamping appeared by telephone for Susan Hogeland (Petitioner).
Brynne Wilson appeared by telephone for Thomas Mazza.

Mr. Lamping stated that the discovery process is in progress, that they may explore mediation after discovery is completed, but mediation is premature at this point and suggested putting the matter out for 60 to 90-days. Ms. Wilson stated that she agrees with Mr. Lamping and that some discovery has been conducted, but she prefers a 90-day continuance. The Court informed Counsel that it is issuing a case management conference order. A case management conference hearing is set for September 29, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. A joint case management statement is to be filed by September 18, 2020. Once the case management conference order is posted on the Court's website, Mr. Lamping is to serve a copy on all Parties and file a proof of service. The Petition to Determine Rights of Omitted Spouse, etc. is continued to September 29, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: J. Reyes. Not reported.
